tests.name: Loading state with no aggregated configuration renders no tokens and no crash buildRegistry returns an empty registry when given an empty aggregated config
Markdown link to a known skill resolves through the registry lookupByName returns the user-scope skill nw-bdd-requirements as a single live entry
Markdown link to a known skill resolves through the registry lookupByPath resolves the absolute markdown link href to the same registry entry
Reference resolving to multiple items renders as an ambiguous token lookupByName for an ambiguous name returns all matching candidates

buildRegistry with 500 items completes synchronously and produces the correct entry count a 500-item AggregatedConfig builds to a registry whose byName.size equals 500 within a generous synchronous budget
RegistryEntry projection preserves identifying fields per RefType a command projects to a registry entry with type='command' and the command's name, scope, source, and filePath
RegistryEntry projection preserves identifying fields per RefType an agent projects to a registry entry with type='agent' and the agent's name, scope, source, and filePath
RegistryEntry projection preserves identifying fields per RefType a hook projects to a registry entry whose name is the basename of its filePath and type is 'hook'
RegistryEntry projection preserves identifying fields per RefType an mcp server projects to a registry entry with type='mcp' and the server's name, scope, source, and filePath
RegistryEntry projection preserves identifying fields per RefType a rule projects to a registry entry with type='rule' and the basename of its filePath as the name
RegistryEntry projection preserves identifying fields per RefType a plugin projects to a registry entry whose source field equals the plugin's name (not its scope)
normalisePath equivalence rules expose the same registry entry a Windows-style backslash path resolves to the same entry as the canonical forward-slash path
normalisePath equivalence rules expose the same registry entry a path with a trailing slash resolves to the same entry as the canonical path
normalisePath equivalence rules expose the same registry entry a path with interior '/./' segments resolves to the same entry as the canonical path
normalisePath equivalence rules expose the same registry entry a path with a leading './' resolves to the same entry as the path without it
normalisePath equivalence rules expose the same registry entry a single '/' path is preserved (the trailing-slash rule does not strip the root)
buildRegistry skips agent parse-error results an aggregated config containing only an agent parse-error produces an empty byName map
buildRegistry skips agent parse-error results when one agent parses and another errors, only the parsed agent appears in lookupByName
byFilePath retains the first entry on path collisions when two entries share a filePath, lookupByPath returns the first one inserted
basename-derived names use the last path segment, not the full path a deeply-nested hook filePath produces a registry name equal to the file's last segment
basename-derived names use the last path segment, not the full path a hook filePath that ends in a separator falls back to the hook command as the name
buildRegistry returns a version strictly greater than prevVersion a rebuild from prevVersion=N produces version N+1, not N or N-1
